current_activity: Single-task backend discovery plan to reconcile MVP backend scope, API ownership, persistence unknowns, and dependency boundaries for the todo application using only the provided planning inputs.
Fast-path discovery plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Plan the frontend discovery lane as a single read-only worker task that captures MVP UI boundaries, frontend-specific unknowns, and consumer-side integration constraints from the provided planning inputs only.
Fast-path discovery plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Plan the middleware discovery lane as one bounded analysis task that identifies MVP integration boundaries, unresolved contract questions, and future cross-lane dependencies without redefining sibling capability ownership.
Fast-path discovery plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Reconciled middleware discovery scope from the injected planning inputs. The middleware lane is bounded to later-phase integration orchestration between a React frontend and a simple backend persistence API, with current discovery limited to identifying shared contract unknowns, sibling dependency assumptions, and future middleware handoffs rather than defining implementation details.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Backend discovery scope was reconciled against the injected planning inputs for the MVP: the backend lane owns serving and persisting todo CRUD data for the React frontend, retains authority over the backend-side API contract for that lane, and defers exact stack and persistence selections to later phases within a simple architecture constraint. Out of scope for this lane are non-MVP concerns such as authentication, multi-user behavior, advanced workflows, and optimization-heavy design.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Frontend discovery boundary is defined as one bounded React MVP lane covering todo create/view/edit/complete/uncomplete/delete behavior, frontend-owned UX constraints, consumer-side API assumptions, and candidate later-phase handoffs.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Single-task backend design reconciliation plan that keeps the lane minimal in design phase by deriving build-ready backend contract guidance from approved planning inputs and prior discovery evidence without creating final lane artifacts or outbound handoffs.
Fast-path design plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.
Single-task frontend design lane that reconciles MVP todo UI behavior, consumer-side API assumptions, and validation criteria from discovery inputs for later build planning.
Fast-path design plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

No standalone middleware design task is planned in this lane because the exact output contract defines no allowed final outputs and no required outbound handoffs for design. The lane remains pending upstream frontend/backend design artifacts and any authoritative integration contract inputs in a later phase.
Fast-path design plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Backend MVP design scope is reconciled from the injected planning inputs and discovery artifacts: the backend lane remains the single authority for the backend-side todo CRUD API contract, uses a simple persistence direction appropriate for a small test application, keeps responsibilities bounded to serving and storing todo item data for the React frontend, and has no unresolved same-phase backend dependencies blocking build planning.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Reconciled the frontend MVP design into one bounded decision set: render a simple single-page todo list with each item shown as a checkbox, text label, and inline action controls; use one persistent input row for creation and switch an individual item into inline edit mode for editing; treat complete/uncomplete as checkbox-driven updates reflected only after API confirmation; confirm delete through an explicit delete action with immediate disabled/loading feedback; require non-empty trimmed text with lightweight inline validation; and define consumer-side loading/error behavior as initial loading state, empty state after successful empty fetch, per-mutation pending disablement, retry-capable fetch error state, and inline mutation error messaging while keeping the backend contract limited to a frontend assumption of a minimal `{id, title, completed}` payload shape.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Plan the frontend MVP lane as three small serial worker tasks: reconcile frontend-only API consumption into notes and a local client, implement the React todo entrypoint and core UI components against that client, then add the MVP test and package the acceptance review bundle.

Lean MVP-build plan with one frontend lane that reconciles frontend-side API assumptions from approved design inputs, delivers the React todo CRUD UI and tests, and hands a concrete review bundle to objective acceptance.

Three serialized backend worker tasks implement the todo persistence layer, wire the approved CRUD API and server entry, then add API-level tests and package the MVP validation and acceptance review handoff.

A single backend lane should carry the MVP todo CRUD API and persistence build from approved design inputs through task-local validation and acceptance handoff, keeping the objective lean and within backend-owned surfaces.

Plan the middleware MVP lane as two serialized worker tasks: one updates the shared workspace manifest and lockfile for the integrated todo application, and the second emits the middleware-owned MVP integration package plus the acceptance review bundle from approved discovery/design inputs and the shared workspace wiring.
Lean middleware-only MVP build outline that reconciles the missing integration contract, owns shared workspace wiring, and emits a single acceptance review bundle.

Implemented the SQLite-backed todo persistence module at the owned path with route-ready CRUD methods, input validation, schema initialization, transaction handling, and minimal runtime verification against create/list/update/delete behavior.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Created the frontend-owned todo API client module at `apps/todo/frontend/src/todos/todoApi.js` and documented the approved frontend-only API consumption assumptions in `runs/phase5-todo-full-013/artifacts/frontend-api-consumer-notes.md`. Validation passed for module export loading and required output file presence.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Created the new todo route module at apps/todo/backend/src/todos/routes.js against the injected SQLite store contract, but the task remains blocked because the required server entry path apps/todo/backend/src/server.js already exists with older bootstrap/repository wiring and the exact task contract forbids existing-file edits, so I cannot legally replace it to complete backend_server_entry.
Execution finished with status blocked.

Added a root `start` script that points at the existing integrated todo runtime entrypoint in `package.json`, then generated a matching npm lockfile (`lockfileVersion: 3`) so the shared app-root workspace wiring is installable and reproducible for the MVP delivery flow.
Codex turn completed.
